title: Automate gated Bureau runtime snapshot refresh
goal: Automatically refresh the immutable Bureau runtime and canonical read-only Registry snapshot after a verified main change, without touching the conventional dirty checkout, weakening deployment gates, or creating duplicate deployments.

acceptance.assertion: A versioned trigger considers only an exact merged origin/main commit with successful required CI and an explicit deployment intent; arbitrary branches, dirty sources, unverified heads and ambiguous GitHub state fail closed.

Refresh uses a clean detached or task-specific checkout at the exact expected origin/main commit and never pulls, resets, stashes, cleans or otherwise mutates the conventional Bureau main checkout or any foreign worktree.
Launcher, Bureau prefix and source checkout are lease-bound; identical in-flight or already-current refreshes are deduplicated, while a conflicting deployment or lease blocks before mutation.

The existing immutable installer runs with an exact-head precondition, preserves rollback evidence, and success requires post-install readback of manifest, package tree, snapshot inventory/tree, launcher digest and canonical bureau --json check.
A failed or unclear refresh remains visible with a durable receipt and does not retry, self-heal, or claim current runtime state until an operator-authorized reconciliation proves the outcome.

A read-only receipt reports origin/main, deployed source_commit, lag in commits and elapsed time; verified Registry changes become visible through the canonical snapshot within a documented bounded interval or produce an explicit alert.

Tests cover already-current dedupe, foreign lease, origin drift, dirty source, failed CI, installer failure, unclear outcome, rollback evidence, successful readback and preservation of a stale dirty conventional checkout.

metadata.source: GRIP-ROADMAP-V1-T014 closeout and live Bureau runtime reconciliation
metadata.observation: The canonical Bureau Registry already marked T014 verified at origin/main 7a07635de3e6, while the active immutable snapshot still pointed to 6f17c5a44143 and reported T014 planned. Manual atomic deployment restored consistency; existing drift and scheduler tasks explicitly exclude automatic repair.
metadata.safety_boundary: Automation may refresh only the immutable Bureau release and canonical read snapshot after all gates pass. It must not mutate Registry task truth, the conventional checkout, state-root data, systemd units or unrelated runtime paths.
